title: Astronauts Come Home After a Big Space Trip
summary: Four astronauts came back to Earth after a special space mission called Artemis II. They traveled far into space, even farther than people have gone before, and saw the back side of the moon. When they landed in the ocean near San Diego, a ship picked them up. Then, they flew to Houston, Texas, where NASA has a space center. Many people, like space workers and families, cheered for them at a big welcome home party. The astronauts felt happy to be back with their families and friends. They talked about how amazing it was to be in space and how special Earth is. This mission happened on the same day as an old space mission called Apollo 13, which had a problem but turned out okay.

background_read: Space missions like Artemis II help us learn about the moon and space. NASA is the group that sends astronauts on these trips. Astronauts train for a long time to go to space, and they use special ships to travel. When they come back to Earth, they land in the ocean and are picked up by a recovery ship. Welcome home parties celebrate their safe return and the things they discovered. The Apollo 13 mission was famous because it had a big problem but the astronauts made it back safely, showing how people can work together in tough situations.
Article_Structure: WHO: Four astronauts named Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen
WHAT: They came back from a space mission called Artemis II, where they traveled far and saw the moon's back side
WHERE: They landed in the ocean near San Diego and then went to Houston, Texas
WHY: To celebrate their safe return and share their experiences from space

perspectives.description: The astronauts felt happy and amazed by their trip, missing their families and appreciating Earth.
